prompt:
Build-engineering ticket. Our internal .props-style config files are key=value lines, and the value side keeps getting corrupted by hand-rolled quoting scattered across three tools — Windows paths lose backslashes, values with spaces get split, and one tool writes literal newlines into the file. I want ONE static class that owns the value encoding: PropsValue, namespace PropsQuote, in a new PropsValue.cs. The xunit tests already in the project are the contract (`dotnet test`); don't modify PropsValueTests.cs, the csproj, or .gitignore.

The format rules, written down at last:

Quote(string value) -> the on-disk form. If the value is non-empty and every character is a letter, a digit, or one of . _ - / : + then it goes out bare, exactly as-is. Anything else gets wrapped in double quotes, with this escaping inside: backslash becomes \\, the double quote becomes \", newline becomes \n, tab becomes \t, carriage return becomes \r; any other character below U+0020, and DEL (U+007F), becomes \uHHHH with exactly four UPPERCASE hex digits; everything else — spaces, #, =, unicode text — is written verbatim inside the quotes. Empty string is two double quotes. Null is an ArgumentNullException, not a format problem.

Unquote(string text) -> the value back. If text starts with a double quote it must be a complete quoted form: ends with the matching unescaped quote, nothing after it, recognizing exactly the escapes above (hex digits accepted in either case on the way in), with a raw control character below U+0020 inside the quotes being an error — those must arrive escaped. If it doesn't start with a quote it must be a valid bare form (non-empty, bare charset only). Anything else — unterminated quotes, trailing junk after the close quote, an unknown escape, a backslash at end of input, a hex escape cut short or with non-hex digits, a bare value with a space or # in it — throws FormatException. Null again ArgumentNullException.

The law the tools actually depend on: Unquote(Quote(v)) == v for every string v — the tests run a table of the values that historically got mangled, including trailing spaces, quotes, tabs, control characters and Windows paths. Stdlib only, no regex golf required.
